text.title: graph-rag Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for graph-rag as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Skipping entity resolution and inserting raw extracted entities directly into the graph.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Duplicate nodes receive separate community assignments; queries against one node miss relationships attached to the duplicate.

failure-mode.description: Running global search inside a ReAct agent loop without result caching.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each iteration burns 2000+ tokens per community summary. Three loop iterations on a corpus with 500 communities costs 3M tokens.

failure-mode.description: Deploying GraphRAG without a human review checkpoint for graph writes from unverified sources.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: One hallucinated relationship from entity extraction can corrupt multi-hop reasoning for all subsequent queries across the affected entities.

failure-mode.description: Relying on Leiden community detection to be deterministic across rebuilds.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Leiden is non-deterministic; rebuilding the index with identical data produces different community structures. Do not use community IDs as stable references.

failure-mode.description: Using LightRAG for legal or medical knowledge graphs requiring precise relationship granularity.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LightRAG's 10x token reduction compresses relationship metadata. Fine-grained distinctions between relationship types are lost.
